commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f69f001c50ea9b4058cccd800f30f857b081683a)
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes embedded-agent model-call diagnostics to compute `responseStreamBytes` from stream chunks with `partial` snapshots removed and adds regression tests for delta-only byte counting plus opaque chunks.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +104. Total +123 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrow diagnostics amplification path: current main serializes the full stream chunk, and standard stream delta events carry an accumulated `partial` snapshot. The broader Windows local-provider starvation issue remains only partially addressed by this PR.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer compatibility/API decision for a shipped diagnostics field, not a safe mechanical repair task.

target.title: fix(agents): count stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes the diagnostics stream-byte counter introduced by `c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f` so plain stream deltas are counted from the new `delta` only
- avoids serializing full `partial` assistant snapshots on every streamed chunk, removing the repeated answer-so-far diagnostic work called out in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599#issuecomment-4555471713
- keeps diagnostic byte inspection best-effort so opaque or hostile chunk shapes cannot break the model stream

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: model-call diagnostics no longer repeatedly stringify full accumulated assistant partial snapshots for ordinary `text_delta`, `thinking_delta`, and `toolcall_delta` stream chunks.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate. Blacksmith Testbox proof was attempted through the Crabbox wrapper but the local Blacksmith CLI was not authenticated, so the broad remote gate used direct AWS Crabbox instead.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json -- corepack pnpm check:changed
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: refreshed head `93807327321`; focused local wrapper passed `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` with 13/13 tests after the final rebase; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings with `overall: patch is correct (0.84)`; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed for the same two-file patch with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_984053942fb9`, slug `tidal-krill`, run `run_b9fa60b707b4`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`. The changed gate selected lanes `core` and `coreTests`.

Observed result after fix: regression coverage proves diagnostic byte accounting does not call `toJSON` on full partial snapshots for text deltas, counts only incremental delta bytes, and keeps streaming alive when byte inspection cannot inspect a chunk.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This removes the diagnostics amplification from the starvation cluster but does not claim to close every related local-provider responsiveness path alone.
target.author: vincentkoc
